macro_segments.title: The Neharde'a versus Sura Versions of the Rav-Shmuel Debate on Invalid Schach

micro_segments.title: Introduction to the two versions and their key differences
The Sura version: both Rav and Shmuel distinguish between side and middle
The Neharde'a version: debate between Rav and Shmuel themselves
Practical ruling: following Shmuel despite normally ruling like Rav

macro_segments.title: Textual Proofs from Mishnayot and Baraitot: Four Tefachim and Planks

micro_segments.title: The plank mishna (Natan aleha neser): how Rav's position explains it
How Shmuel's position requires reinterpreting the mishna
The baraita on sheets versus planks combining
Testing Rav's approach: why planks must combine to four amot on the side
Testing the Sura version of Rav: resolving the apparent contradiction
Baraita on cedar planks: four tefachim universally invalid
Rabbi Meir's concession: kosher schach between invalid planks allows validity
Rav Huna's solution: alternating planks on an eight-amah sukkah

macro_segments.title: Abaye's Ruling on Small Sukkot and Gaps: Three Tefachim in Minimum-Sized Structures

micro_segments.title: Abaye's position: three tefachim of invalid schach divides a small sukkah
Abaye's reasoning: space definition in minimum-sized sukkot

macro_segments.title: The Debate on Levud in the Middle of a Roof: Rav Acha versus Ravina

micro_segments.title: Gemara raises whether levud applies in the middle like on the side
Rav Acha: levud applies everywhere, even in the middle of a roof
Ravina: levud does not apply in the middle of a roof
Proof for levud in middle: beams not touching in a chatzer
Counter-proof against levud in middle: tumah laws and skylights
Gemara response: tumah laws are exceptional, not general rule

macro_segments.title: Rabbi Yehuda ben Rabbi Ilai's Public Teaching and Qualifications

micro_segments.title: First teaching: a hole in the roof with schach remains valid
Rabbi Yishmael ben Rabbi Yosi's challenge: qualification by four amot needed
Second teaching: the fish avruma is permissible
Rabbi Yishmael's challenge on avruma: depends on location
Ravina's addendum: nowadays all fish are forbidden due to river changes

macro_segments.title: Sukkah on a Portico with Posts: Introduction to Pi Tikra Yored Ve-Sotem

micro_segments.title: Case: sukkah on a portico with columns (patzimin)

macro_segments.title: Sukkah on a Portico with Posts: Introduction to Pi Tikra Yored Ve-Sotem (continued)

micro_segments.title: Abaye: valid even without usable posts via pi tikra yored ve-sotem
Rava: invalid; pi tikra yored ve-sotem does not apply
Explanation: roof edge as virtual wall descending

macro_segments.title: Abaye-Rava Debate and Its Relation to Rav-Shmuel on Shabbat

micro_segments.title: Question: does Abaye-Rava debate reflect Rav-Shmuel positions?
Shabbat case: roofed portico in a valley
Rav on Shabbat: pi tikra yored ve-sotem works
Shmuel on Shabbat: only four amot work, not pi tikra
Gemara analysis: Shmuel consistently rejects pi tikra
Abaye's concession: two-wall scenario (mavoy hamefulash)
Rava's distinction: roofed versus unroofed portico affects pi tikra
Final reasoning: roof edge must serve the enclosed space below

topical_tags.term: Pi tikra yored ve-sotem / Roof edge descends and closes
